what_it_does: Implements the Almanac room: a generic JSON-face reader that loads surface/atlas.json, lists faces in a sticky shelf, fetches individual face JSON via C.api.dna(name), and renders any face structure as titled sections, rows, paragraphs, lists, cards, and quiet provenance lines, with in-face search, a proofs drawer, and estate stats.
observations: Self-contained IIFE that registers a DNA room with id 'almanac'.
Fetches surface/atlas.json at mount and builds the face shelf, proofs panel, and stats strip.
Renders arbitrary face JSON generically using helpers like section, entryBlocks, rowsOf, cards, and renderArray.
Distinguishes provenance keys (evidence, status, notes, etc.) and renders them as faint mono lines.
Implements client-side search over the currently open face with section-level and unit-level filtering.
Clears the face cache on every enter() so edits elsewhere are reflected.
Includes a large embedded CSS block for layout, typography, and mobile adaptation.
Uses C.esc for escaping, C.api.dna for data, C.org.icon/detailStrip for chrome, and C.navigate for the Gallery link.
standouts: Generic recursive renderer that turns any JSON shape into readable documentation panels without schema knowledge.
Quiet apparatus/provenance rendering keeps metadata visible but subordinate.
Search index rebuilt after each render and filters sections and nested cards or panels smoothly.
Proofs drawer is a separate, collapsible report of the registry's proof list.
conventions: Follows the DNA.rooms.register contract with mount/enter/leave lifecycle.
Uses a ctx object C for shell-provided utilities and API calls.
Escapes all interpolated strings via C.esc.
Prefixes CSS classes with 'al-' to namespace the room.
Uses CSS custom properties from the shell (--dna-*, --charcoal, etc.).
concerns: The CSS block is large and embedded as a string, making it harder to lint or reuse.
Search uses textContent.toLowerCase() which can be slow for very large faces.
selectFace falls back to the first face when a hinted or requested face is missing, which may surprise users.
isQuiet only checks primitive top-level provenance keys, so an object-valued provenance key still becomes a numbered section.
No explicit cleanup of event listeners or timers on leave or mount reuse.
notes: A reading-room renderer: it does not own the data, it opens whatever the atlas registry serves and presents it as a browsable document.
questions: What is the exact shape of atlas.faces entries and atlas.proofs entries?
How does C.api.dna(name) resolve the JSON path and what errors does it throw?
What does DNA.ordinal(i, n, token) return and how is it seeded?
Is C.org.icon always available and what icon names exist?
How are room unmount and remount handled by the shell?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the comments describe it as a generic reading room for any face JSON, and the implementation recursively renders arbitrary face documents, lists faces from the atlas, and provides search and provenance display.
apparent_themes: Provenance and evidence as first-class but quiet citizens
Live data: fresh on every arrival
intention_signals: Build a single, stable place where every 'face' of the language can be read and searched without writing a custom page per face; keep the scholarly apparatus visible but unobtrusive.
novelty: A schema-agnostic JSON-to-document renderer that treats provenance keys as a separate visual register, plus integrated in-document search with animated filtering.
contribution: Provides a reusable room that turns the atlas registry and face JSON into a navigable, searchable reading interface, including generic rendering, face selection, search filtering, proofs drawer, and responsive styling.
summary_for_embedding: The Almanac room is a generic JSON-face reader that loads an atlas registry, lists faces, fetches face content, and renders arbitrary structured documents as sections, rows, cards, and quiet provenance lines with in-page search and a proofs panel.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to be the single reading room where every face of the language can be opened, browsed, and searched without custom per-face templates.

symbols.does: Strips leading underscores and converts underscores to spaces in a key string.
symbols.description: Normalizes JSON object keys for human-readable labels by removing leading underscores and replacing underscores with spaces.

symbols.does: Converts a cleaned key to title case, leaving small words lowercase after the first word.
symbols.description: Turns a normalized key into a display title by capitalizing words except a defined set of small words when they are not first.

symbols.does: Returns true if a key names a provenance or scholarly-apparatus field.
symbols.description: Identifies keys that should be rendered as quiet provenance lines, including note, evidence, status, provenance, cross_ref, checker, invariant, evidence_class, and class.

symbols.does: Returns true for null or non-object values.
symbols.description: Used to decide whether an array contains only leaf values that can be rendered inline.

symbols.does: Renders a primitive value as HTML, with special handling for null, hex colors, numbers, and booleans.
symbols.description: Converts a scalar value into escaped HTML; hex strings get a color swatch, numbers and booleans use mono styling, and null becomes an em dash.

symbols.does: Builds a quiet apparatus line for a label/value pair, recursively flattening objects.
symbols.description: Renders provenance metadata as a faint mono row; object values are flattened with dot-joined labels and arrays become dot-separated strings.

symbols.does: Builds a label/value HTML row.
symbols.description: Returns a div pairing a label with already-generated value HTML, used for flat key/value presentation.

symbols.does: Builds a labeled paragraph block.
symbols.description: Returns a div with a small uppercase label and a paragraph element, used for longer string values.

symbols.does: Renders an array of primitives as chips or a list.
symbols.description: Chooses a compact chip layout for short items or an unordered list for longer items, rendering each element via leaf.

symbols.does: Recursively flattens nested objects into indented label:value rows.
symbols.description: Walks an object beyond the first nesting level, producing rows or provenance lines with prefix paths to keep the trail readable.

symbols.does: Renders an array of objects as a grid of cards.
symbols.description: Each object becomes a card using an id or name title if present, with remaining fields rendered through entryBlocks.

symbols.does: Renders an array value according to its contents and nesting depth.
symbols.description: Dispatches primitive arrays to primList, top-level object arrays to cards, and deeper object arrays to indented rows.

symbols.does: Renders an object's entries as sections, panels, rows, or deep blocks depending on depth.
symbols.description: At depth 0 it turns objects into titled panels; at depth 1 it uses rowsOf for nested objects, while strings, numbers, booleans, and arrays get appropriate row or list treatment.

symbols.does: Renders a top-level face key as a numbered section with an ordinal swatch.
symbols.description: Wraps a key/value pair in a section element with a heading, ordinal color dot, two-digit number, and recursively rendered body.

symbols.does: Safely calls C.tok() if it exists, returning null on error.
symbols.description: Defensive accessor for the shell token function used when generating ordinal colors.

symbols.does: Renders the header for a face: path, title, version pill, lede, author, and meta provenance.
symbols.description: Builds the article header from face.meta and the atlas entry, including a special note with a link to the Gallery for the 'tokens' face.

symbols.does: Renders a complete face into the article element and wires up search.
symbols.description: Builds the header and sections, skips quiet primitive provenance keys from numbering, inserts HTML, triggers enter animation, and rebuilds the search index.

symbols.does: Returns true for top-level primitive provenance keys in the current face.
symbols.description: Local predicate used inside renderFace to exclude quiet provenance keys from the section count and numbering.

symbols.does: Selects a face by name, fetches it if not cached, and renders it.
symbols.description: Validates the name against the atlas, updates the shelf selection, clears search, fetches face JSON via C.api.dna, and handles stale deep-link fallback and request sequencing.

symbols.does: Indexes sections and their nested units for text search.
symbols.description: Collects each section element, its full text, its own non-nested text, and per-card or per-panel unit text so applyFilter can hide non-matching parts.

symbols.does: Filters the article based on the search input.
symbols.description: Hides sections that do not match the query; when a section matches but the query is only in nested units, it hides non-matching units, and updates the result count.

symbols.does: Builds the left navigation list of faces.
symbols.description: Creates buttons for each atlas face with title-cased name and description, wiring click handlers to selectFace.

symbols.does: Builds the collapsible proofs panel.
symbols.description: Renders a toggle button showing the proof count and a list of proof run/is entries, with expand and collapse behavior.

symbols.does: Builds the estate stats detail strip.
symbols.description: Displays counts of faces, documents, proofs, and registry version using C.org.detailStrip.
